title: Human Agent Guide — Handling Calls in the Contact Center
lede: A complete, step-by-step guide for a human agent who answers and makes calls in iMash. No technical knowledge needed — just follow along. You'll learn how to log in, go online, answer and place calls, read the caller's record, transfer calls to a teammate or team, update the lead, add notes, and wrap up.

steps.title: Step 1 — Log in to your account
steps.content:
1. Open the link your company gave you in your web browser (Chrome works best) — this is your login page.
  2. Type the email and password your manager sent you.
  3. Tick Remember me so you don't have to sign in every time on your own computer.
  4. Click Sign in.

Tip: if you forget your password, use Forgot password or ask your manager to reset it — don't create a second account.

images.caption: Your login page — enter the email and password your manager gave you.
Your login page — enter the email and password your manager gave you.

steps.title: Step 2 — Get to know your home screen
steps.content:
After you log in you land on the Contact Center — this is your home base all day. Here's what you see:

  • Left menu — Contact Center, Do Not Call, and your CRM (Accounts, Contacts, Leads). At the bottom are Account Settings and Logout.
  • On Queue (middle) — customers waiting to be connected to an agent show up here.
  • Web dialer (right side) — your phone. It stays open all the time so you never miss a call.

images.alt: Agent home screen with the web dialer open
images.caption: Your home screen: the Contact Center on the left, the queue in the middle, and your always-on web dialer on the right.

images.alt: Agent home screen with the web dialer open
images.caption: Your home screen: the Contact Center on the left, the queue in the middle, and your always-on web dialer on the right.

steps.title: Step 3 — Meet your web dialer
steps.content:
The web dialer on the right is your phone. From top to bottom:

  • A number field and a keypad to dial a phone number.
  • Call — place a phone call to the number you typed.
  • Text — send an SMS instead of calling.
  • AI Agent — hand the call to an AI assistant.
  • Teammate — call another agent on your team (their dialer rings).
  • My status — set yourself Online / On Break / Offline.

images.caption: Your web dialer: keypad, Call, Text, AI Agent, and Teammate.
Your web dialer: keypad, Call, Text, AI Agent, and Teammate.

steps.title: Step 4 — Go Online so calls reach you
steps.content:
1. Find My status at the bottom of the dialer.
  2. Set it to Online — now you'll be rung for inbound calls to your team's numbers and for calls the AI hands to your team.
  3. Use On Break when you step away, and Offline at the end of your shift.

Important: if your status is On Break or Offline, calls will NOT ring you. Set yourself Online at the start of every shift.

images.caption: Set My status to Online to start receiving calls. Use On Break / Offline when you step away.
Set My status to Online to start receiving calls. Use On Break / Offline when you step away.

steps.title: Step 5 — Set two handy options (gear icon)
steps.content:
Click the gear next to My status to open Web dialer settings:

  • Auto-answer — connects group calls automatically, so you don't have to click Accept every time. Great for busy queues.
  • Auto-open lead/contact — automatically opens the caller's record when an incoming call matches a lead or contact, so their details are on screen before you say hello.

Ask your manager which options they'd like you to turn on.

images.caption: Web dialer settings: Auto-answer and Auto-open lead/contact.
Web dialer settings: Auto-answer and Auto-open lead/contact.

steps.title: Step 6 — Answer an incoming call
steps.content:
When a call comes to you, a small incoming-call card pops up in the dialer showing who's calling.

  1. Click Accept (the green button) to take the call — or Decline if you can't.
  2. If Auto-answer is on, the call connects on its own — just start talking.
  3. For a team (group) call, the first agent to Accept wins the call; the others are released automatically, so answer quickly.

Once connected, the caller's record opens on screen (see the next step).

images.alt: Answering a call in the dialer
images.caption: When a call rings you, click Accept in the dialer (or it auto-connects if Auto-answer is on).

images.alt: Answering a call in the dialer
images.caption: When a call rings you, click Accept in the dialer (or it auto-connects if Auto-answer is on).

steps.title: Step 7 — Read the caller's record while you talk
steps.content:
When a call connects, the caller's Lead Details open so you can see who you're talking to. Look at:

  • The name, phone number and email at the top.
  • The Lead Information tab — company (Account), tags, and fields.
  • The Additional Information and Call Logs tabs for history and past calls.

Use this to greet the customer by name and pick up where the last conversation left off.

images.caption: The caller's Lead Details — name, phone, email, and tabs with their full history.
The caller's Lead Details — name, phone, email, and tabs with their full history.

steps.title: Step 8 — Update the customer's stage
steps.content:
Scroll to the Lifecycle bar on the lead and click the stage that matches where the customer is now:

  • New → Contacted → Interested → Qualified → Negotiation → Won.
  • Some stages have a small arrow — click it to pick a more specific sub-stage (for example "No Answer" or "Interested").

Keeping the stage up to date tells your team and your manager exactly where each customer stands.

images.caption: The Lifecycle bar — click the stage that matches the customer after your call.
The Lifecycle bar — click the stage that matches the customer after your call.

steps.title: Step 9 — Write a note about the call
steps.content:
1. On the lead, find the Notes box ("Add your note").
  2. Type a short, clear note about what happened — for example "Called back, wants a quote for 2 doors, follow up Friday."
  3. Press Enter / Save. Your note is stamped with your name and the time, so anyone who talks to this customer next knows the story.

Good notes are the #1 thing that makes you look professional to the next agent and to your manager.

images.alt: Add a note on the lead
images.caption: Add a short note about the call so the next person knows what happened.

images.alt: Add a note on the lead
images.caption: Add a short note about the call so the next person knows what happened.

steps.title: Step 10 — Transfer the call to a teammate or team
steps.content:
If someone else should handle the call, transfer it instead of hanging up:

  • Teammate — click Teammate, pick a colleague, and their dialer rings. (Only teammates who are Online with their dialer open appear in the list.)
  • To a team / skill (queue) — send the customer to the right team so the next available specialist answers.
  • AI Agent — hand the call to the AI assistant.

You can add the other person while staying on the line (a warm transfer / conference — you introduce them), or transfer and drop off. If you leave a queued call, the AI keeps the customer company until someone picks up.

images.caption: Transferring to a teammate — pick who should take the call; their web dialer rings.
Transferring to a teammate — pick who should take the call; their web dialer rings.

steps.title: Step 11 — Make an outbound call
steps.content:
1. Type the phone number on the keypad (or open a lead and click its phone number).
  2. Click Call.
  3. In the Start Call window, pick the Caller ID (the number the customer will see) and, if asked, an AI Agent to join the call.
  4. Click Call to place it. Talk when the customer answers.

images.caption: Placing an outbound call — choose the Caller ID the customer will see, then Call.

steps.title: Step 12 — Review a call afterwards
steps.content: Every call you handle is saved under the customer's Call Logs tab, with the date, direction, duration and (if enabled) the recording and transcript. Open it any time to remind yourself what was said, or to show your manager how a call went.

images.alt: Call Logs tab on the lead
images.caption: The Call Logs tab keeps a record of every call with this customer.

images.alt: Call Logs tab on the lead
images.caption: The Call Logs tab keeps a record of every call with this customer.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
